abbott-greg.text: Greg Abbott (Republican) is a candidate for Governor of Texas.

On taxes, Greg Abbott's recorded position: Abbott has made property tax reduction a centerpiece of his governance, signing over $18 billion in property tax cuts in 2023 and seeking at least $10 billion more. He advocates for eliminating school property taxes for homeowners and raising business personal property exemptions.
Greg Abbott is the incumbent in this race. Recorded background: Greg Abbott, incumbent governor (2015–present).
As of 2026-02-21, Greg Abbott's campaign had raised $0 in campaign receipts and spent $42,593, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

hinojosa-gina.text: Gina Hinojosa (Democratic) is a candidate for Governor of Texas.

On education, Gina Hinojosa's recorded position: Hinojosa is a strong advocate for public school funding and opposes school vouchers. She authored an opinion piece explicitly stating she opposes the governor's voucher plan, calling it 'irresponsible and harmful.' She emphasizes that Texas public schools need at least $40 billion to reach national per-student funding averages and has worked with bipartisan allies to…
Gina Hinojosa's recorded background: Gina Hinojosa, state representative from the 49th district (2017–present).
As of 2026-02-21, Gina Hinojosa's campaign had raised $951,069 in campaign receipts and spent $673,405, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

dixon-pat.text: Pat Dixon (Libertarian) is a candidate for Governor of Texas.

On education, Pat Dixon's recorded position: Dixon strongly supports school choice, endorsing statewide voucher programs where education funding follows students to any school. He criticizes standardized testing mandates and 'Robin Hood' school funding redistribution, citing the Texas voucher plan that accepted 90,000 students as a model to expand rather than restrict.
Pat Dixon's recorded background: Pat Dixon (Libertarian), former chair of the Libertarian Party of Texas
As of 2025-12-31, Pat Dixon's campaign had raised $4,050 in campaign receipts and spent $0, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

raphoon-jenn.text: Jenn Mack Raphoon (Independent) is a candidate for Governor of Texas.
Jenn Mack Raphoon's recorded background: Jenn Mack Raphoon (Independent)
As of 2025-06-30, Jenn Mack Raphoon's campaign had raised $0 in campaign receipts and spent $0, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total).

base.summary: Governor of Texas race: Greg Abbott, Gina Hinojosa, Pat Dixon, Jenn Mack Raphoon. This overview lists each candidate's party, background, and campaign finance where available in our data set.
base.caveats: Our data set for this race covers candidate name, party, incumbency, background summary, and campaign finance totals where a filing was found (federal candidates file with the FEC; Texas state offices file with the Texas Ethics Commission -- each finance bullet names which). We do not have recorded voting histories or issue-by-issue policy positions for these candidates in this data set.

now.text: On taxes, Greg Abbott's recorded position is: "Abbott has made property tax reduction a centerpiece of his governance, signing over $18 billion in property tax cuts in 2023 and seeking at least $10 billion more. He advocates for eliminating school property taxes for homeowners and raising business personal property exemptions." That's where Greg Abbott stands right now, this term.

long_term.text: If Greg Abbott's recorded position on taxes holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping this issue the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Greg Abbott keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

now.text: On education, Gina Hinojosa's recorded position is: "Hinojosa is a strong advocate for public school funding and opposes school vouchers. She authored an opinion piece explicitly stating she opposes the governor's voucher plan, calling it 'irresponsible and harmful.' She emphasizes that Texas public schools need at least $40 billion to reach national per-student funding averages and has worked with bipartisan allies to block voucher legislation." That's where Gina Hinojosa stands right now, this term.

long_term.text: If Gina Hinojosa's recorded position on education holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping this issue the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Gina Hinojosa keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

now.text: On education, Pat Dixon's recorded position is: "Dixon strongly supports school choice, endorsing statewide voucher programs where education funding follows students to any school. He criticizes standardized testing mandates and 'Robin Hood' school funding redistribution, citing the Texas voucher plan that accepted 90,000 students as a model to expand rather than restrict." That's where Pat Dixon stands right now, this term.

long_term.text: If Pat Dixon's recorded position on education holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping this issue the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Pat Dixon keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

abbott-greg.text: On housing, Greg Abbott's recorded position: Abbott explicitly advocates for housing affordability through deregulation, cutting regulations, and speeding up permitting to make it easier to build. He also proposes a one-year tax exemption on home improvements.

abbott-greg.text: On taxes, Greg Abbott's recorded position: Abbott has made property tax reduction a centerpiece of his governance, signing over $18 billion in property tax cuts in 2023 and seeking at least $10 billion more. He advocates for eliminating school property taxes for homeowners and raising business personal property exemptions.

On education, Greg Abbott's recorded position: Abbott is a leading proponent of school choice, having signed Texas's landmark Education Savings Account program in 2025. He campaigned on 'education freedom,' supports diverting public funds to private options through ESAs, and emphasizes parental control over education decisions.

As of 2026-02-21, Greg Abbott's campaign had raised $0, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total) -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on property taxes or public school funding.

hinojosa-gina.text: On education, Gina Hinojosa's recorded position: Hinojosa is a strong advocate for public school funding and opposes school vouchers. She authored an opinion piece explicitly stating she opposes the governor's voucher plan, calling it 'irresponsible and harmful.' She emphasizes that Texas public schools need at least $40 billion to reach national per-student funding averages and has worked with bipartisan allies to…
On taxes, Gina Hinojosa's recorded position: She calls for ending corporate loopholes and using the proceeds to lower middle-class taxes while protecting neighborhood-school funding.

As of 2026-02-21, Gina Hinojosa's campaign had raised $951,069, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total) -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on property taxes or public school funding.

dixon-pat.text: On education, Pat Dixon's recorded position: Dixon strongly supports school choice, endorsing statewide voucher programs where education funding follows students to any school. He criticizes standardized testing mandates and 'Robin Hood' school funding redistribution, citing the Texas voucher plan that accepted 90,000 students as a model to expand rather than restrict.

On taxes, Pat Dixon's recorded position: Dixon states that no party wants lower taxes more than the Libertarian Party, insists spending cuts must precede tax reductions, and opposes replacing property taxes with higher sales taxes without local control.

As of 2025-12-31, Pat Dixon's campaign had raised $4,050, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total) -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on property taxes or public school funding.

hinojosa-gina.text: On healthcare, Gina Hinojosa's recorded position: Hinojosa has consistently supported expanding government healthcare coverage. She voted to extend Medicaid eligibility for 12 months after birth or miscarriage (HB133) and supported a pilot program for Medicaid coverage of doula services (HB158). Her campaign platform emphasizes fighting for affordable health care and strengthening health care access.

As of 2026-02-21, Gina Hinojosa's campaign had raised $951,069, per Texas Ethics Commission filings (this filing period only, not a cycle-to-date total) -- fundraising scale only, not a stance on health coverage, the ACA, or Medicaid.

That's what people focused on higher education funding or student costs can expect from Greg Abbott this term.

long_term.text: If Greg Abbott's recorded position on education holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping higher education funding or student costs the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Greg Abbott keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

now.text: On education, Gina Hinojosa's recorded position is: "Hinojosa is a strong advocate for public school funding and opposes school vouchers. She authored an opinion piece explicitly stating she opposes the governor's voucher plan, calling it 'irresponsible and harmful.' She emphasizes that Texas public schools need at least $40 billion to reach national per-student funding averages and has worked with bipartisan allies to block voucher legislation." That's what people focused on higher education funding or student costs can expect from Gina Hinojosa this term.

long_term.text: If Gina Hinojosa's recorded position on education holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping higher education funding or student costs the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.
long_term.assumption: Assumes Gina Hinojosa keeps this position and it turns into lasting policy, rather than changing later or staying just talk.

now.text: On education, Pat Dixon's recorded position is: "Dixon strongly supports school choice, endorsing statewide voucher programs where education funding follows students to any school. He criticizes standardized testing mandates and 'Robin Hood' school funding redistribution, citing the Texas voucher plan that accepted 90,000 students as a model to expand rather than restrict." That's what people focused on higher education funding or student costs can expect from Pat Dixon this term.

long_term.text: If Pat Dixon's recorded position on education holds and becomes sustained policy, it would likely keep shaping higher education funding or student costs the same way over the next decade. This is a projection, not a guarantee.

rural_agriculture.summary: For a rural agriculture voter looking at the Governor of Texas race (Greg Abbott, Gina Hinojosa, Pat Dixon, Jenn Mack Raphoon): our data set does not contain candidate-specific positions or voting records on farming, ranching, water rights, or rural infrastructure for this race. Below is what we do have on each candidate's background and, where available, campaign finance.
rural_agriculture.caveats: We only show a connection to rural agriculture concerns when a candidate's own recorded background in our data set names it directly (for example, a farming or small-business background). Where no such record exists, we say so rather than guess at a candidate's likely position.
